name: checklist
context: Prepublish checklist - naming, tree links, entry scripts, search terms, and smoke tests
info: A pragmatic checklist to run after validation passes and before release. This node exists under a skill on purpose to demonstrate that context nodes may live under executable parents, and it emphasizes the things that still break real releases even after JSON validation passes.
triggers: what should i check before publishing
body:
# Prepublish Checklist

Use this checklist after validation passes and before release.

1. Confirm `uri`, `parent`, and `type` match the intended tree.
2. Confirm `aliases`, `triggers`, and `tags` match real user phrasing.
3. Confirm `body`, `sections`, `links`, and `related` teach something useful and point at real sections.
4. Confirm entry scripts, schemas, and tests still match the manifest surface.
5. Confirm the node appears in search, tree, and learn routes during a local smoke test.
6. Only then move to [[dojo/publish/local]] or [[dojo/publish/registry]].

sections.title: Structural checks
sections.body: Check `name`, `version`, `uri`, `type`, and `parent` together. Most runtime surprises start with these fields drifting apart, especially when a node moves and its links or section-target references do not move with it.

sections.title: Entry scripts and tests
sections.body: If a manifest points at `scripts[].entry`, confirm the file still exists, still matches the manifest description and schema, and still has tests appropriate to the claimed lifecycle status. A node marked `verified` should not drift away from the executable behavior its tests cover.

sections.title: Search checks
sections.body: Read the aliases and triggers out loud. If they sound like internal jargon rather than real user phrasing, improve them before release.

sections.title: Learn checks
sections.body: Load the node through `/v1/learn/*` and inspect the reading path, links, backlinks, and sections. Check at least one section-target link directly. If it feels thin or broken there, it will feel thin or broken to an agent too.

sections.title: Release checks
sections.body: Confirm the version is correct and immutable, then hand off to [[dojo/publish]].

links.context: Contribution workflow that uses this checklist
What a strong learn payload should look like

author: dojo-community
